AI Capability Built for Higher Education

Move your staff and students to confident, capable and compliant AI practitioners. Hours saved, tracked and reported.

AI training, coaching and consultancy by those who fully understand universities.

Book Your AI Capability Check

A free, confidential 45-minute call followed by a written summary report.

Built by former Digital Skills Leads from UK universities.
Proud members of the Staff Development Forum.

  • SDF

Services

AI support designed for every part
of your institution

Whether you need to lift staff capability, equip students for an AI-shaped workplace, coach an individual academic, or give your leadership team a confident strategic position, we can design something for you.

Workforce Capability

Your professional services and academic staff using AI in real work, not just sitting through a session. Built role by role, mapped to tasks they already do, with time savings tracked from day one.

Learn more →

Student Programmes

Students feel confident in understanding how to use AI responsibly in their studies and the wider world of work. Programmes support your academic integrity policy and employability strategy at the same time.

Learn more →

Leadership Consulting

A clear strategic advisory to increase AI capabilities and engagement in your staff and students. Designed for SLT, department and student services heads, who need to lead on this, not just respond to it.

Learn more →

Academic Coaching

One-to-one and small-group coaching for academics developing their own AI practice across research, teaching and admin. Confidential and paced to fit a real academic schedule.

Learn more →

Events and Away Days

A sector-aware introduction to AI for conferences, leadership days and team events. One session that moves a room from uncertainty to a clearer sense of what AI means for their work.

Learn more →
See all our services →

Our flagship offering

The AI Partnership

For institutions that want to stop running pilots and start joining up the dots. Over an academic year, move from uncertainty to genuine capability - with everything designed around your roles, your policies and your calendar.

  • ✓ Free initial consultation and baseline assessment
  • ✓ AI literacy audit and needs analysis
  • ✓ Role-specific workshops, in-person or online
  • ✓ On-demand courses for staff and students
  • ✓ Strategic roadmap session with your SLT
  • ✓ Ongoing AI Café drop-in sessions throughout the year
  • ✓ Follow-up leadership calls to track progress and adjust
Discuss the AI Partnership

Every partnership is scoped to the institution. Book an AI Capability Check to start that conversation.

Who we work with

Built for the people who own AI adoption

We work with the people tasked with turning AI curiosity into
institution-wide confidence, capability and compliance.

HR and People Directors

You need staff who feel supported through this change - not left to navigate tools that shift every few weeks.

Learning and Development Leads

You need AI capability building that works for the full range of staff you support, from lecture theatres to finance offices.

Digital and IT Leaders

You need AI adoption that's measurable and policy-aligned, not a set of tools your staff are left to work out on their own.

Professional Services Leaders

You need your teams to reclaim time from repeatable work, without sacrificing quality or professional judgement.

Academic Leaders

You need AI confidence grounded in academic values - one that takes integrity, research practice and the realities of teaching seriously.

“Students overwhelmingly see AI as essential for their futures, but many do not feel adequately supported to develop the necessary skills. If universities want graduates to feel prepared for the future, then AI literacy and capability must be embedded across the curriculum. These skills cannot be treated as optional.”

Charlotte Armstrong, Policy Manager, Higher Education Policy Institute - HEPI/Kortext Student Generative AI Survey 2026

AI maturity

Five stages of AI capability
in higher education

AI adoption happens in stages. These are the five we see most often. We support you at each.

  1. 01

    Recognising the strategic priority

    AI is on every board agenda. You know you need a coordinated response, not a series of individual experiments.

  2. 02

    Understanding your starting point

    Before you build a plan, you need an honest picture of staff capability, current usage and risk exposure across the institution.

  3. 03

    Building the institutional plan

    Translating insight into a board-ready roadmap with measurable outcomes, accountable owners and a realistic timeline.

  4. 04

    Equipping staff with confidence and capability

    Role-specific training that respects the difference between an academic, a finance officer and a student services lead.

  5. 05

    Embedding and sustaining

    AI evolves quickly. Your institution needs structures that keep momentum after the initial push.

Not sure which stage you’re at? Book your AI Capability Check →

“Students overwhelmingly see AI as essential for their futures, but many do not feel adequately supported to develop the necessary skills. If universities want graduates to feel prepared for the future, then AI literacy and capability must be embedded across the curriculum. These skills cannot be treated as optional.”

Charlotte Armstrong, Policy Manager, Higher Education Policy Institute - HEPI/Kortext Student Generative AI Survey 2026

We’ve worked inside the buildings we’re trying to help.

Our team brings over a decade of direct experience inside UK higher education, and more than four decades combined across learning, educational technology, and learning and development. That context shapes every workshop we design and every conversation we have with your team.

We know about Clearing pressures. NSS reporting cycles. OfS conditions and condition B. Academic integrity policy at programme level. The gap between schools and professional services. The honest reasons why “just send them an email” doesn’t get staff engagement. The reality of academic scepticism when something new feels imposed.

This isn’t theory for us. It’s the world we came from.

Meet the team →

ROI calculator

What does the status quo cost you?

Unstructured AI use isn’t free. Staff are spending hours working out tools and prompts that someone else has already worked out. This calculator shows what a coordinated approach could reclaim.

How much could you save with AI?

  3. 2 Set your average salary
  5. 3 Choose your adoption level

Your institution could reclaim £0 this year, the equivalent of freeing up 0 full-time staff. That's the gap between unstructured AI use and a coordinated approach. We close it.

Free resource

From chatbot to agent in one task.

A short guide that takes a single task most staff already do, and shows how it can be improved with AI - from a quick AI prompt to a full agentic workflow. See how AI capability works at different levels, depending on workforce capability.

Download the guide

Where does your institution stand?

Book your AI Capability Check

A confidential 45-minute conversation, followed by a written summary report on where your institution sits and the three things to do next. No pitch, no slides, no obligation.

Book Your AI Capability Check
